content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

ROBERT R. WELLINGTON
ROBERT R. WELLINGTON
KENNETH D. BUCHERT

805 ABREGO STREET

MONTEREY, CALIFORNIA 93940
(408) 373-8733

March 20, 1990


Kathy Donovan, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California  95815

                                    Re:  City of Marina;
                                         Request for Advice Letter

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     I am the City Attorney for the City of Marina and I would
like to request an advice letter on the following question:

     "For the purposes of 2 California Code of
Regulations 18703.1(a), does the City of
Marina have jurisdiction over that por-
tion of its population which resides on
Fort Ord?"

     The reason for this question lies in the relationship of
the City of Marina and Fort Ord, an active United States Army
installation.  The City, population 16,733, is located on the
western boundary of Fort Ord and several years ago, pursuant
to state and federal law, the City annexed a portion of Fort
Ord with a current population of 13,380.  The City, including
the annexed area, therefore has a total population of 30,113
and a geographic area of 9.6 miles.  On the surface it would
seem that the condition of Regulation 18703.1(a), relating to
a population of 25,000 or less, is not applicable, except that
the City has absolutely no control over the population and ter-
ritory found on Fort Ord.  The City does not provide municipal
services to the area, such as fire or police protection, does
not control development, zoning or building standards, and does
not collect property taxes from the area.  In short, the area
and the population residing within it are not subject to any
of the laws of the City of Marina.

'Kathy Donovan, Esq.        March 20, 1990             Page 2


     The only benefit the City derives from the annexation is
that the City receives certain tax subventions from the state
which are based on population, such as motor vehicle in lieu,
gasoline and cigarette taxes. As federal property, the area
is not subject to the City's transient occupancy tax, prop-
erty transfer tax, planning and land use/building inspection
fees and the sales and use tax on retail sales. The residents
of the annexed area are, however, eligible to vote in City of
Marina elections, and 1,702 residents exercised that option
at the last municipal election.

     The issue,then, is whether the above-described relation-
ship means that the City of Marina has "jurisdiction" for pur-
poses of Regulation 18703.1(a) over that population residing
on Fort Ord. A sub-issue would be that even if the elected
City Council has such "jurisdiction", does the Council-ap-
pointed Planning Commission, which exercises no authority over
the population or territory within the geographic limits of
Fort Ord, have "jurisdiction" for the purposes of Regulation
18703.1(a).

     The answer to this question is extremely important to the
City because with the relatively small area of the City it is
not unusual to have several members of the City Council or
Planning Commission who are so disqualified from participation in a deci-
sion due to the proximity of their interest in real property.
In fact, at a recent meeting of the Planning Commission, three
of the seven members were so disqualified and a fourth was dis-
qualified due to another reason, resulting in use of the rule
of necessity just to achieve a quorum. This situation is quite
common in the City.

     I would appreciate whatever advice you can provide on this
question, and if you need any further information on this matter
please feel free to contact this office.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert R. Wellington
                                        Marina City Attorney

RRW:pb
cc:   City Manager
      City Planning Director

sections.question: For the purposes of 2 California Code of Regulations 18703.1(a), does the City of Marina have jurisdiction over that portion of its population which resides on Fort Ord?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the City of Marina has jurisdiction under California Code of Regulations 18703.1(a) over the population residing on Fort Ord, an annexed federal military installation over which the City exercises no governmental control or provides no services.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains the detailed question but no FPPC conclusion or advice. A sub-question regarding Planning Commission jurisdiction is also noted.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Marina requests an FPPC advice letter regarding whether the City has jurisdictional authority under Regulation 18703.1(a) over an annexed portion of Fort Ord's population that receives no municipal services despite being included in the City's population count for tax revenue purposes.
